seoTitle: Recover a Deleted Transaction in QuickBooks
metaDescription: Use the QuickBooks Online Audit Log to trace a deleted transaction's original details and rebuild it accurately.
intro: An accidentally deleted invoice or expense doesn't have to be a permanent loss. This guide shows how to use QuickBooks Online's Audit Log to find the exact details of a deleted transaction and rebuild it in your books.

steps.heading: Open the Audit Log
steps.body: Click the gear icon in the top right and select Audit Log to open the system history.

steps.heading: Filter for deleted transactions
steps.body: Click Filter, set your target date range, and select Deleted/Voided Transactions under the Events row.

steps.heading: Apply the filter
steps.body: Click Apply to narrow the log down to your timeline of interest.

steps.heading: Review the transaction details
steps.body: Click View next to your lost transaction to expand the audit history and see original debit and credit rows.

steps.heading: Rebuild the transaction
steps.body: Open a matching blank form, such as an Invoice or Expense, and re-enter the date, category, and amount from the audit log, then save.

faq.question: Can QuickBooks automatically restore a deleted transaction?
faq.answer: No, there's no one-click restore; you use the Audit Log to view the original details and manually re-enter the transaction.

faq.question: What information does the Audit Log show for deleted transactions?
faq.answer: It shows details like item descriptions, classes, tax rates, dates, and the exact debit and credit accounts used.

faq.question: How far back does the Audit Log go?
faq.answer: It generally covers your account's full history, though you'll want to set date filters to narrow your search.

faq.question: Should I void instead of delete transactions going forward?
faq.answer: Voiding keeps a visible record while zeroing out the financial impact, which can simplify future reconciliation compared to permanent deletion.
